pattern: Treat each string as a graph state and breadth-first search over swaps, but only ever fix the first mismatched position by swapping in a character that belongs there, which keeps the branching small while guaranteeing the minimum swap count.
hints: Each swap should make progress, so always target the leftmost position where the two strings still differ.
From a given arrangement, only consider swaps that place the correct character into that leftmost wrong spot; this prunes useless moves while preserving optimality.
Run a breadth-first search whose states are partially-corrected strings; the depth at which you first reach the target is the minimum number of swaps.

edge_inputs.description: two independent swaps, not a single chain
repeated letters allow a cheaper alignment
a long cycle costing length-minus-one swaps

stress.description: random anagram pairs over the six-letter alphabet; s2 is a shuffle of s1 so it is always a valid anagram
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 12))
    alphabet = 'abcdef'
    chars = [rng.choice(alphabet) for _ in range(n)]
    s1 = ''.join(chars)
    shuffled = chars[:]
    rng.shuffle(shuffled)
    s2 = ''.join(shuffled)
    return [s1, s2]
stress.note: kept short because the BFS branches; s2 is a permutation of s1 by construction
